steps.title: Use the productivity contrast
steps.content: The study contrasts people who did easy tasks first with those who did hard tasks first. The hard-task-first group became more skilled and productive over time, so the easy-task-first group was less productive or effective by comparison.

steps.title: Choose the work-related word
steps.content:
• Choice A: Describes hiding information, not productivity.
  • Choice B: Means productive and effective, matching the study outcome.
  • Choice C: Describes sociability, not work performance.
  • Choice D: Describes lack of sympathy, unrelated to task results.
The answer is B.
